abstract: A key assumption in data science is that the fairness of an algorithm depends on its accuracy. Antecedents that create accuracy problems are expected to reduce fairness and cause algorithmic social injustices. We theorize why complexities in ground truths, IT ecosystems, and statistical models of algorithms can also generate algorithmic social injustices, above and beyond the indirect effects of antecedents, through the mediation of accuracy problems. We also theorize technology design and organizational mitigation mechanisms for taming such complexities and reducing algorithmic social injustices. We tested the proposed theory in a sample of 363 matched pairs of problematic and problem-free algorithms. We found that complexities in ground truths affected algorithmic social injustices directly rather than through the mediation of accuracy problems. Failures in complex IT ecosystems of algorithms did not affect the likelihood of algorithmic social injustices, but they caused damage directly and indirectly through the mediation of accuracy problems. Failures in complex statistical models significantly increased algorithmic social injustices both directly and indirectly through the mediation of accuracy problems. The results indicate that agentic algorithms produce social injustices not only through accuracy problems but also through complexities in their ground truths, IT ecosystems, and statistical models. The proposed complexity taming mechanisms are effective in reducing algorithmic social injustice risks through (1) the user organization’s quality in managing the algorithm’s stakeholders, (2) designing algorithms with a large scope of human-like interaction capabilities, (3) the developer organization’s algorithmic risk mitigations, and (4) the user organization’s algorithmic risk mitigations.

affiliation: The University of Texas at Austin
authors_full.name: Hüseyi̇n Tanriverdi̇

affiliation: The University of Texas at Austin
authors_full.name: John-Patrick Olatunji Akinyemi

ob.ai_reason: 算法公平性与社会不公，核心AI议题，IS/伦理相关
ob.tldr: 算法公平性不仅取决于准确率。研究检验了363对问题算法与正常算法的匹配样本，发现真实情况复杂性、IT生态系统故障、统计模型复杂性均会直接导致算法社会不公，而非仅通过准确率间接传导。统计模型故障的影响最为显著。复杂度管控机制在降低算法社会不公方面有效，表明算法代理性本身即可产生社会不公。

title: Algorithmic Social Injustices: Antecedents and Mitigations
title_zh: 算法社会不公：成因与缓解措施
